scope.mode: read-only static audit plus safe exclusion harnesses
scope.execution: No target code executed; target working tree remained clean.

observations: POST /api/evolve has no authentication or scope check and accepts caller-controlled code, tests, language, generation count, and population size (app/api/evolve/route.ts:38-76).

POST /api/seal-bundle accepts caller-controlled bundle contents and applies the server HMAC key without authentication, ownership lookup, or binding to a persisted job (app/api/seal-bundle/route.ts:41-80).

POST /api/save-run accepts caller-supplied orgId and bestGene data, persists them to DSQL, and writes unbounded request-derived run data to public/demo-data/runs (app/api/save-run/route.ts:19-29,38-69,80-120).

JavaScript sandbox evaluates attacker-controlled code with eval inside node:vm; the documented context restriction is not itself a security boundary (app/api/sandbox/javascript/route.ts:16-18,102-187).
Python timeout uses Promise.race around Pyodide execution; timeout rejection does not terminate the underlying execution (app/api/sandbox/python/route.ts:120-138,250-255).
Python sandbox loads Pyodide from a configurable CDN URL without integrity verification (app/api/sandbox/python/route.ts:69-76).

hypotheses.statement: Unauthenticated callers can mint server-HMAC-valid fabricated evolution bundles.
hypotheses.basis: The sealing route accepts caller-provided contents and signs them with PHYLO_HMAC_KEY without authenticate(), persistence lookup, or verification against a server-owned job.
hypotheses.reason: Static evidence only; no target runtime or signing endpoint was exercised.

hypotheses.statement: Unauthenticated or cross-tenant callers can forge saved runs or attribute genes to an arbitrary org.
hypotheses.basis: save-run has no authentication and uses body.orgId directly for DSQL writes; bestGene and test counts are client-controlled.
hypotheses.reason: Static evidence only; no database mutation or HTTP request was executed.

hypotheses.statement: The JavaScript sandbox may permit VM escape or resource exhaustion through attacker-controlled eval payloads.
hypotheses.basis: User code and tests are evaluated in node:vm; timeout is only synchronous script execution protection and input sizes are not capped at the route boundary.
hypotheses.reason: Testing escape payloads would execute target-provided code, which was prohibited.

hypotheses.statement: Python sandbox requests can continue consuming resources after the HTTP timeout fires.
hypotheses.basis: Promise.race rejects on timeout but does not cancel runPythonAsync or Pyodide work.
hypotheses.reason: Static evidence only; no Pyodide execution was performed.

deductions: A server-side HMAC does not establish provenance when the signed payload is wholly supplied by an unauthenticated client; it establishes authenticity of the server signer, not truth of the bundle contents.
Client-supplied org identity and winner metadata cannot safely authorize billing, ownership, or durable audit records.
Any sandbox escape or non-cancelled execution would be reachable from unauthenticated /api/evolve unless deployment-level controls exist outside the inspected target.
evidence: artifact: read-only exclusion harness stdout, 2026-07-15; 70 included readable text files, 0 binary, 0 files over 1 MiB, 1 minified/generated metadata artifact excluded, 14 unterminated JSON candidates excluded, excluded-path hits recorded for .git/.gitignore/node_modules/.next/dist/build/cache/__pycache__.

adi.statement: The unbound sealing API is a plausible provenance-forgery path.
If save-run is reachable as deployed, body.orgId and winner fields can influence durable tenant-owned records without server authentication.
The inspected sandbox design does not provide executed evidence of escape resistance.
adi.status: abstained with an exact reason: executing escape payloads or arbitrary target code was prohibited

adi.statement: Promise.race timeout rejection alone cannot prove cancellation of the underlying Pyodide computation.
